steps.title: Preparations to do before starting the...
steps.instruction: Preparations to do before starting the RNA extraction (in the morning of extraction): Chill centrifuge to 4° C Chill ethanol, Trizol LS, and chloroform at -20 o C. Clean workstation and equipment with ethanol and RNAse Away (or any other RNAse decontamination solution) Get a box of ice to keep samples on

steps.title: Sample Preparation Sample extraction procedure differs...
steps.instruction: Sample Preparation Sample extraction procedure differs slightly depending on sample type. For sample from pure culture or enrichment culture ⇒ do step 2.1-2.5 For sample from anaerobic digester ⇒ do step 2.6-2.8

steps.title: The sample volume required to obtain...
steps.instruction: The sample volume required to obtain sufficient RNA yield varies significantly depending on the sample type. The amounts provided here are based on mesophilic batch cultivation of syntrophic acid degrading enrichment cultures at the final stage of substrate degradation. For each sample 3x50 ml were taken and put into 3 50ml falcon tubes while flushing with N 2 # Tip: Try to be as quick as possible and keep samples on ice when possible.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by pouring/gently flicking the tube
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by pouring/gently flicking the tube. Put the pellets on ice. #Tip, try to remove as much liquid as possible without losing the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 750 µl...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 750 µl chilled Trizol LS, mix by pipette. Transfer solution to the next 50ml falcon tube from the same sample, redo mixing and lastly transfer to the last 50ml falcon tube and mix througly. 750 µL, Trizol LS #Discussion In the case of flocculating enrichment cultures, the final pellet volume is quite large after pooling. Therefore the more concentrated Trizol LS is used which requires a lower ratio between the sample and Trizol LS as compared to normal Trizol. The guideline is either 0.75ml Trizol LS or 1ml Trizol for 0.25ml of Sample Volume. You also have to make sure make sure that the final volume should fit in the bead beating tube at the next step. The final volume of pellet + Trizol should be ≈ 1000µl. The absolute maximum that is possible to add to the bead beating tube is ≈1400µl.

steps.title: Transfer the mixed sample to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the mixed sample to a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube ZR BashingBeadTM Lysis Tubes (0.1 & 0.5 mm) Zymo Research Continue at step 3.

steps.title: Homogenize samples using a bead-beater at speed 6
steps.instruction: Homogenize samples using a bead-beater at speed 6.0 for 40 s 00:00:40, Speed 6.0 Warning: Mark the side of the BashingBead Lysis Tube as the bead-beating will wear away any markings made on the top of the tube.

steps.title: Transfer up to 800 μl of...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 800 μl of the mixture into a Zymo-Spin™ IIICG Column in a clean 2.5 ml Eppendorf tube and centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 60 seconds. Save the flow-through! 5000 rcf, °C, 1 minute #Tip: Column might need to be loaded multiple times depending on sample volume.

steps.title: To the flow-through, add an equal...
steps.instruction: To the flow-through, add an equal volume ethanol (95-100%) (1:1) and mix well. 1 Volume, 95-100% Ethanol #Tip: A 2.5ml Eppendorf tube may be too small if the supernatant volume was large. In this case just transfer to RNase free 5ml Eppendorf tube or divide the volume in half and use two 2.5ml tubes.

steps.title: Transfer the mixture into a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the mixture into a new Zymo-Spin™ IIICG Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through. 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds #Column might need to be loaded multiple times depending on sample volume. Just repeat this step in that case.

steps.title: Add 400 µl RNA Prep Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µl RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge fo 30s. Discard the flow-through. 400 µL, RNA Prep Buffer 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds

steps.title: Transfer the column into a nuclease-free tube (not provided)
steps.instruction: Transfer the column into a nuclease-free tube (not provided). Add 100 µl DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the column matrix and centrifuge for 2 minutes at 5000 rcf. 100 µL, DNase/RNase-Free Water 5000 rcf, °C, 2 minutes

steps.title: Zymo-Spin™ III-HRC Filter preparation
steps.instruction: Zymo-Spin™ III-HRC Filter preparation: Place a Zymo-Spin™ III-HRC Filter in a new Collection Tube and add 600 µl Prep Solution. Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 4 minutes. 600 µL, Prep Solution 8000 rcf, °C, 3 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the eluted RNA (step 11)...
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluted RNA (step 11) into the prepared filter in a nuclease-free tube (not provided) and centrifuge at 16,000 x g for 4 minutes. 100 µL, Eluted RNA 16000 rcf, °C, 4 minutes

steps.title: Add 200 µl RNA Binding Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl RNA Binding Buffer to the filtered RNA (step 13) (2:1) and mix well. 200 µL, RNA Binding Buffer

steps.title: Add an equal volume ethanol (95-100%) (1:1) and mix well
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume ethanol (95-100%) (1:1) and mix well. 300 µL, 100% ethanol

steps.title: Transfer the mixture into a new...
steps.instruction: Transfer the mixture into a new Zymo-Spin™ IC Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through. 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds IMPORTANT: At this point, when the RNA has bound to the IC Column, DNA can be removed using DNase I treatment. See 16.1 - 16.3

steps.title: Following RNA binding step (step 16),...
steps.instruction: Following RNA binding step (step 16), add 400 µl RNA Wash Buffer to the column, centrifuge and discard the flow-through. 400 µL, RNA Wash Buffer 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds

steps.title: For each sample to be treated,...
steps.instruction: For each sample to be treated, prepare DNase I Reaction Mix (see table below) in an RNase-free tube (not provided) and mix by gentle inversion. For each sample: 35 µl DNA Digestion Buffer 5 µl DNase I

steps.title: Add 40µl of the DNase I...
steps.instruction: Add 40µl of the DNase I Reaction Mix to the column matrix and incubate at room temperature (20-30°C) for 15 minutes. Proceed with the purification (Step 17) 40 µL, DNase I reaction mix 00:15:00 Room temperature TIP: If you are quantifying RNA concentration using Bioanalyzer take out the kit to let it come up to room temperature at this point.

steps.title: Add 400 µl RNA Prep Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µl RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through. 400 µL, RNA Prep Buffer 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds

steps.title: Add 700 µl RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µl RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through. 700 µL, RNA Wash Buffer 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds

steps.title: Add 400 µl RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µl RNA Wash Buffer and centrifuge the column for 1 minute to ensure complete removal of the wash buffer. Then carefully, transfer the column into a nuclease-free tube (not provided). 400 µL, RNA Wash Buffer 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 1 minute #Tip: The centrifugation step can be repeated without addition of Wash Buffer to ensure that all Wash Buffer is removed.

steps.title: Add 15 µl DNase/RNase-Free Water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 15 µl DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the column matrix, incubate for 2 minutes and centrifuge. 15 µL, DNase/RNase-Free Water (Amount can be varied) 00:02:00, Incubation 10000-16000 rcf, °C, 30 seconds Alternatively, for highly concentrated RNA use ≥ 6 µl elution. The eluted RNA can be used immediately for QC control or stored frozen. The RNA can now be frozen in the -80 ° freezer. Its recommended to do a QC control using Bioanalyzer at this point to avoid repeated freeze/thawing of the RNA samples. Alternatively you can stop here and aliquot 2µl of each sample, freeze everything and continue to do a quality check at a later stage.

steps.title: Info
steps.instruction: Info: For QC control the BioAnalyzer was used in combination with the Agilent RNA 6000 Nano Kit. The protocol followed can be found here ( Protocol ). For each sample to be analyzed 1 µl of eluted RNA is needed. 1 µL, RNA sample Check whether there is fresh filtered Gel prepared or if new gel has to be prepared (Lasts 4 weeks)

steps.title: Decontaminate the Electrodes
steps.instruction: Decontaminate the Electrodes: Slowly fill one of the wells of an electrode cleaner with 350 µl RNaseZAP. Open the lid and place the electrode cleaner in the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. Close the lid and leave it closed for about 1 minute. Open the lid and remove the electrode cleaner. Label the electrode cleaner and keep it for future use. You can reuse the electrode cleaner for all 25 chips in the kit. Slowly fill one of the wells of another electrode cleaner with 350 µl RNase-free water. Place the water-filled chip in the Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer, close the lid and leave for 10s. Remove the chip and leave the lid open for 10s to dry the electrode.

steps.title: Run the analysis according to manufacturers instructions
steps.instruction: Run the analysis according to manufacturers instructions. Use the assay " Prokaryote Total RNA Nano Series II"

steps.title: Reagents and storage requirements for RNA...
steps.instruction: Reagents and storage requirements for RNA depletion Stored in freezer: LA - Linear acrylamide RP - riboPOOL lyophilized,(aliquoted by user) RNAse inhbitor RiboLock (40u/µl) Stored in fridge : SMB - Streptavidin-coated magnetic beads (SMB) Stored in room temperature: DB - Depletion buffer HB - Hybridization buffer SA - Sodium acetate, 3M H2O - Nuclease-free water Reagents that has to be prepared in advance: BRB - Bead Resuspension Buffer 0,1 M NaOH 0.05M NaCl BWB - Bead Wash Buffer 0.1M NaCl

steps.title: Hybridization of riboPOOL to rRNA To...
steps.instruction: Hybridization of riboPOOL to rRNA To 14 μl of RNA sample (containing 100 ng - 5 μg of total RNA), add: If sample volume is > 14 μl, adjust HB volume accordingly to 0.25X total volume. Total volume however should not exceed 40 μl. 1 µL, resuspended RP 5 µL, Hybridization Buffer (HB) X µL, RNase inhibitor (RiboLock) #Tip: For RNase inhibitor the final concentration should be 1 U/μl. Given a RiboLock concentration of 40 U/μl and a working volume of 20 μl (14+1+5) -> 0.5μl of RiboLock should be added to each sample

steps.title: Clean up by EtOH precipitation Can...
steps.instruction: Clean up by EtOH precipitation Can be done either over night at -20°C, or for 30 min at -80°C. -20 °C Overnight Or -80 °C 00:30:00

steps.title: Vortex and spin down droplets
steps.instruction: Vortex and spin down droplets

steps.title: Incubate at 68°C for 10 min to denature RNA
steps.instruction: Incubate at 68°C for 10 min to denature RNA. 00:10:00 68 °C

steps.title: Allow to slowly cool down from...
steps.instruction: Allow to slowly cool down from 68°C to 37°C for optimal hybridization. To do this turn off the heat block and let it naturally fall down to 37°C

steps.title: Preparation of beads Resuspend the streptavidin-coated...
steps.instruction: Preparation of beads Resuspend the streptavidin-coated magnetic beads (SMB) by carefully vortexing tube at medium speed.

steps.title: For each sample transfer 90 μl...
steps.instruction: For each sample transfer 90 μl bead suspension into a fresh tube. For batch washing of beads for multiple samples, aliquot bead suspension for up to 6 (i.e. 540 μl) or 12 samples (i.e. 1080 μl) in a single tube. 90 or X µL, bead suspension

steps.title: rRNA depletion is performed with the riboPOOL Kit
steps.instruction: rRNA depletion is performed with the riboPOOL Kit. For more detailed information read the protocol that comes with the kit. The protocol presented here is based on the kit protocol. Newest protocols found here: https://www.sitoolsbiotech.com/protocols.php The current protocol is based on the following: https://www.sitoolsbiotech.com/pdf/riboPOOLkitManualv1-6_2023.pdf

steps.title: Place tube on magnetic rack and...
steps.instruction: Place tube on magnetic rack and wait for 1 min 00:01:00

steps.title: Aspirate and discard all supernatant, while...
steps.instruction: Aspirate and discard all supernatant, while keeping the tube on the magnetic rack.

steps.title: Add 80 μl per sample (i
steps.instruction: Add 80 μl per sample (i.e. 480 μl for 6 samples, 960 μl for 12 samples ) of Depletion Buffer (DB) and agitate the tube well to resuspend beads. Place on magnetic rack (1 min), aspirate and discard supernatant. 80 µL, Depletion Buffer (DB) 00:01:00

steps.title: (The following steps follow the Quick-RNA™...
steps.instruction: (The following steps follow the Quick-RNA™ Fecal/Soil Microbe Microprep protocol, starting from step 4.) #### OBS##### All centrifugation for RNA extraction should be done at 10,000 -16,000 rcf at room temperature unless stated otherwise. Phase separation after centrifugation. Example of sample from enrichment culture. Note: For samples from digesters, the aqueous phase will have a brown color. T ransfer 400 µl (or as much as available) of the aqueous phase into an RNase-free tube (not provided) and add 1 volume of RNA binding buffer. Mix well. X(400) µL, Supernatant X(400) µL, RNA binding Buffer #Tip: If more than 400 µl can be extracted do that, but you will need to repeat step 7 in order to filter all of the liquid.

steps.title: Add 200 µl chilled chloroform to the cell mixture
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl chilled chloroform to the cell mixture. Mix well by inversion. 200 µL, Chloroform

steps.title: Centrifuge the mixture in pre-cooled centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the mixture in pre-cooled centrifuge at 4° C, 14000 rcf for 10 minutes. 14000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Info
steps.instruction: Info: For QC control the BioAnalyzer was used in combination with the Agilent RNA 6000 Nano Kit. The protocol followed can be found here ( Protocol ). For each sample to be analyzed 1 µl of dissolved RNA is needed. 1 µL, RNA sample Check whether there is fresh filtered Gel prepared or if new gel has to be prepared (Lasts 4 weeks)

steps.title: Run the gel according to manufacturers...
steps.instruction: Run the gel according to manufacturers instructions Use the assay " mRNA Nano Series II"

steps.title: Add 1 ml chilled TriZol Reagent (alt
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml chilled TriZol Reagent (alt. 750 μL TriZol LS) 1 mL, TriZol Reagent Continue at step 3.

steps.title: Add 200-250 μl of the sample...
steps.instruction: Add 200-250 μl of the sample to a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube. Put tube on ice. ZR BashingBeadTM Lysis Tubes (0.1 & 0.5 mm) Zymo Research Depending on the viscosity and particle size, samples can either be transferred by pipetting or scooping. Determine the volume using a scale.

steps.title: Centrifuge in pre-cooled centrifuge at 4°...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge in pre-cooled centrifuge at 4° C, 5500 RPM for 30 minutes 5500 rpm, 4 °C, 30 minutes

steps.title: Sample the process at a specified time-point
steps.instruction: Sample the process at a specified time-point. Ensure the workstation is clean and ready before sample collection. As RNA degrades quickly in the samples, try to work fast and immediately proceed to the next step.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 32 μl...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 32 μl of nuclease-free water (H2O) or appropriate elution buffer for library preparation. If required, elution volume can be decreased down to 10 µl. X µL, (Or whatever your final end volume should be)

steps.title: Dry pellet at room temperature for 5 min
steps.instruction: Dry pellet at room temperature for 5 min

steps.title: Repeat ethanol wash (steps 29
steps.instruction: Repeat ethanol wash (steps 29.6 to 29.8)

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard all supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard all supernatant, making sure not to disrupt pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 11 000 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 11 000 x g for 5 min at 4°C 11000 rcf, 4 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Add 200 μl of 70% ethanol to wash pellet
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl of 70% ethanol to wash pellet. 200 µL, EtOh (70%)

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard all supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard all supernatant, making sure not to disrupt pellet.

steps.title: AFTER INCUBATION
steps.instruction: AFTER INCUBATION: Centrifuge at 11 000 x g (or max speed) for 30 min at 4°C 11000 rcf, 4 °C, 30 minutes, (Or at max speed)

steps.title: Incubate tube at -80°C for 30 min OR -20°C overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate tube at -80°C for 30 min OR -20°C overnight

steps.title: Resuspend beads in 80 μl per sample (i
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in 80 μl per sample (i.e. 480 μl 6 samples) of Depletion Buffer and agitate the tube well to resuspend beads. 80 µL, Depletion Buffer (DB)

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the tube containing ~20...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the tube containing ~20 μl hybridized riboPOOL and total RNA (from step 26.3) to spin down droplets.

steps.title: Combine 80 µl of prepared beads (from step 27
steps.instruction: Combine 80 µl of prepared beads (from step 27.5) with ~20 μl of the hybridized riboPOOL-RNA solution. Agitate the tube to resuspend well. 80 µL, prepared beads 20 µL, hybridized RNA

steps.title: Incubate at 37°C for 15 min,...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37°C for 15 min, followed by a 50°C incubation for 5 min. 37 °C, 15 min 50 °C, 5 min

steps.title: Briefly spin down droplets
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down droplets. Place on magnet for 2 min. Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new tube. 00:02:00, Magnetic Rack OBS: This new tube is where RNA will be precipitated, make sure to use the ones provided by the kit in order to get a clear visible pellet. If you are running the optional step below make sure to have this kind of tube for step 28.5

steps.title: Place the new tube with the...
steps.instruction: Place the new tube with the supernatant on the magnetic rack for 1 min to get rid of trace amounts of beads. (optional) 00:01:00, Magnetic Rack

steps.title: Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new tube (optional)
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new tube (optional) . At this point, RNA can be stored at -20°C overnight or -80°C for up to a month according to the procol, however it's advisable to proceed with the RNA Purification step(29).

steps.title: Add 10 µl of 3M sodium...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µl of 3M sodium acetate (SA), 1 µl of Linear acrylamide (LA) and 333 µl of 100%ethanol to the RNA solution from step 28.5 Sample 10 µL, Sodium Acetate (SA) 1 µL, Linear acrylamide (LA) 333 µL, EtOh (100%) Vortex the mixture well

steps.title: Decontaminate the Electrodes
steps.instruction: Decontaminate the Electrodes: Slowly fill one of the wells of an electrode cleaner with 350 µl RNaseZAP. Open the lid and place the electrode cleaner in the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer. Close the lid and leave it closed for about 1 minute. Open the lid and remove the electrode cleaner. Label the electrode cleaner and keep it for future use. You can reuse the electrode cleaner for all 25 chips in the kit. Slowly fill one of the wells of another electrode cleaner with 350 µl RNase-free water. Place the water-filled chip in the Agilent 2100 bioanalyzer, close the lid and leave for 10s. Remove the chip and leave the lid open for 10s to dry the electrode.

steps.title: Run a quality control on the depleted RNA
steps.instruction: Run a quality control on the depleted RNA.
